hypothesis: If the CNN-GRU-attention architecture is extended into a tandem inverse design framework where the decoder is conditioned on a target binding free energy, then simultaneously optimizing both polymer sequence and surface spatial pattern through gradient-based or evolutionary search in the learned latent space will reveal non-intuitive synergistic sequence-pattern combinations that achieve prescribed adhesion strengths unattainable by optimizing polymer sequence or surface composition independently, thereby demonstrating that the joint sequence-pattern design space contains emergent adhesion regimes not predictable from the marginal effects of either design variable alone.
